text:
**1. [Blocker тАФ Phase E] The Trellis redaction hook leaks resource keys through the audit log.** `redact_trace` (crates/trellis-testing/src/scenario.rs:280-292) redacts `resource_commands`, `resource_coalescences`, and invariant names тАФ but not `trace.audit_log`, and `AuditEvent::ResourceOpenCoalesced` carries a full `ResourceKey` (crates/trellis-core/src/transaction_types.rs:87-94). The epic cites these hooks as the redaction foundation; as implemented, a "shareable" capsule would ship un-redacted keys. Suggested change: add a Phase E checklist item тАФ "upstream trellis fix: `redact_trace` must cover `audit_log` (and any future key-carrying fields); add a test that serializes a redacted capsule and asserts no original key material survives anywhere in the JSON." The "all prerequisites closed" claim is true of the listed issues, but this gap is new. [...] Assistant: Finding 1 confirmed: `redact_trace` covers `resource_commands`, `resource_coalescences`, and `invariant_results` тАФ the `audit_log` is untouched. Checking the audit event and versioning claims:

Assistant: **Yes тАФ and it earned its keep.** The Fable reviewer (run locally, since fabric @fable never came online) returned 17 findings тАФ 4 blockers, 8 should-fixes тАФ and it didn't just critique my prose: it read the actual Trellis source and caught things the plan's "all prerequisites closed" framing had papered over. I verified the sharpest claims in the code myself before acting on them, and both held:

**The two claims that mattered most (both confirmed):**
1. **Trellis's redaction hook leaks resource keys.** `redact_trace` covers resource commands, coalescences, and invariant names тАФ but the audit log is cloned into every trace un-redacted, and `AuditEvent::ResourceOpenCoalesced` carries a full `ResourceKey`. A "shareable" capsule built on those hooks would ship the very keys you thought you'd scrubbed. Filed as [trellis#161](https://github.com/pablof7z/trellis/issues/161), now a hard gate for Phase E. [...] **6. [Should-fix тАФ Phase A/B] Type lints can't catch string leakage, and Phase B's "reason string" is exactly that hole.** Debug-formatting a `ResourcePlan` or `ResourceKey` into a reason string leaks Trellis vocabulary invisibly to any type-based gate. Suggested change: replace "reason string" with structured reason codes (NMP enum + typed params, rendered to text at the edge), plus a snapshot/regex test that rendered receipts contain no `trellis`/`ResourcePlan`/`NodeId(`-pattern substrings. [...] **Other findings that changed the epic:** capsules need a **symbolication manifest** (traces are keyed by opaque NodeIds тАФ unreadable to anyone who didn't see the session without an embedded label registry); Trellis trace versioning is exact-match-only, so the capsule needs its own **envelope version + compat policy** or every capsule attached to an old issue dies on the first format bump; the Phase D **transport decision moved into Phase A** because the lint-exemption wording depends on it; free-form reason strings became **structured reason codes** (type lints can't catch a Debug-formatted `ResourcePlan` leaking into a string); the receipt shape gained the fields its own examples required (owner-count *transition*, outcome status, parent scope, timestamps); my "agent answers why-is-my-feed-empty" exit criterion was rightly called untestable and is now a **seeded failure-scenario corpus** against a local relay fixture тАФ deliberately including the "subscription fine, zero events came back" case, which receipts alone can't answer; and the **headless NMP host runner** is now itemized as its own (probably largest) Phase C work item. [...] 2. **X-Ray (the critical-path decision gate):** *"Work Phase A of NMP epic #2858: draft the ADR-0075 amendment for #2809 тАФ define the diagnostic surface, decide the Phase D transport (dev-only declared projection vs side devtools channel) and record it in the ADR, and implement the doctrine-lint allowlist ratchet (crates permitted to depend on `trellis-*`) plus the string-leak snapshot test."* Everything else in that epic queues behind this ADR. [...] **7. [Should-fix тАФ Phase B] The receipt shape is missing fields its own cause-chain examples require.** The shape bullet (projection key, view/scope label, interest shape, owner key, owner count, relay rows, reason string) omits: transaction id + revision + sequence number (the first Phase B bullet promises them тАФ unify); wall-clock timestamp (`TransactionTrace` has none; NMP must stamp it); command kind (open/replace/refresh/close); outcome/status (a CLOSE that failed at the socket looks identical to one that succeeded); owner-count **transition** (the epic's own example is "refcount 2тЖТ1", but the shape only has a current count); parent-scope reference (teardown cascades ordered "children first" need hierarchy); and a structured cause/parent link for `why_subscription_open` to return. Also flag "interest shape" explicitly as the primary privacy-bearing field тАФ it's a Nostr filter containing pubkeys/event ids, and Trellis redaction never sees it.
